{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11552732]] Wikidata name: Ōnoyama Park [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:49, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenceded|date=February 28, 2014 (Fri) 11:11 (UTC)|sort key=Okinawa Ounoyama Park}} {{Park | Name = Okinawa Prefectural Onoyama Park | English Name = | image = [[File:Ounoyama Park2.JPG|300px]] | image Caption = <center>Multipurpose Field (photographed September 11, 2009)</center> | Country = {{JPN}} | City = [[:en:Okinawa Prefecture|Okinawa Prefecture]][[:en:Naha|Naha]] Onoyama-cho | Latitude Degree = 26 | Latitude Minute = 12 | Latitude Second = 12.0 | N/S = N | Longitude Degree = 127 | Longitude Minute = 40 | Longitude Second = 30.95 | E/W = E | Map = Japan Naha city#Okinawa Main Island | Classification = {{ill|Sports park|ja|運動公園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sports park|qq=|1=sports park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16363669}} | Area = 24.99 [[Hectares|ha]] | Predecessor = | Opened = {{ill|June 1959|ja|1959th year 6月|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 1959|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | Operator = Trust Tech Co., Ltd. | Annual Visitors = | Status = Open year-round | Facilities = See [[#Facilities]] | Parking = Available (3 locations, total 385 spaces) | Accessibility = | Architect/Engineers = | Announcement = | Office = Onoyama Park Management Office | Office Address = 52 Onoyama-cho, Naha, Okinawa Prefecture | Phone Number = 098-858-2700 | Remarks = | Official Site = {{Official|http://www.ounoyama.jp/}} }}
'''Okinawa Prefectural Onoyama Park''' (沖縄県営奥武山公園, '''Okinawa Ken'ei Ounoyama Kōen''') is a {{ill|Sports park|ja|運動公園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sports park|qq=|1=sports park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16363669}} located in [[:en:Naha|Naha]], [[:en:Okinawa Prefecture|Okinawa Prefecture]].
== Overview ==
Established in {{ill|June 1959|ja|1959th year 6月|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 1959|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as Okinawa Prefecture's first sports park, it serves as the main venue for the annual {{ill|Naha Festival|ja|那覇まつり|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naha Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 那覇大綱挽まつり|1=Naha Great Tug-of-War Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642807}} and the {{ill|Okinawa Industrial Festival|ja|沖縄の産業まつり|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okinawa Industrial Festival|qq=|1=Okinawa Industrial Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11552591}} held every October.
* '''Management Office:''' Onoyama Comprehensive Sports Park Management Office (located within the Martial Arts Hall) * '''Address:''' 52 Onoyama-cho, Naha, Okinawa Prefecture * '''Classification:''' Sports park * '''Area:''' 24.99 ha<ref>[http://www3.pref.okinawa.jp/site/view/contview.jsp?cateid=213&id=13974&page=1 Okinawa Prefecture Civil Engineering and Construction Department Onoyama Park]</ref> * '''Facilities:''' [[:en:Pitch (sports field)|Athletics stadium]], [[:en:Baseball field|Baseball field]], [[:en:Tennis court|Tennis courts]], {{ill|Martial arts hall|ja|武道館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Martial arts hall|qq=|1=budōkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16534385}}<ref>The Okinawa Prefectural Onoyama Gymnasium, which existed before the construction of the Martial Arts Hall, was demolished to make way for the current facility.</ref>, {{ill|Archery range|ja|弓道場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Archery range|qq=|1=kyūdōjō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487113}}, [[:en:Swimming pool|Swimming pool]], among others. * '''Designated Manager:''' Trust Tech Co., Ltd.<ref>[http://www3.pref.okinawa.lg.jp/site/contents/attach/19486/list.pdf Facilities Implementing the Designated Manager System in Okinawa Prefecture (PDF)]</ref> (Headquarters: 303 Orokku, Naha, Okinawa Prefecture)
== Usage Fees ==
Fees vary depending on the time slot but generally average around ¥8,000 for the athletics stadium, under ¥5,000 for the baseball field, and about ¥2,000 for the swimming pool. These are usage fees when no general admission fees are charged. See the official site for more details.
== Facilities ==

* '''Capacity:''' Approximately[[Category:11 to pp]] 9,000 (turf stands) * '''Certification:''' Not certified by the Japan Association of Athletics Federations * '''Track:''' 400m, 8 lanes * '''Additional Facilities:''' Auxiliary track (dirt)

In February 2013, Naha City announced plans to rebuild the athletics stadium as a soccer-specific stadium meeting {{ill|J-League Club Licensing Standards|ja|Jリーグクラブライセンス制度|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/J-League Club Licensing Standards|qq=|1=J-League Club Licensing Standards|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11227156}}. '''Okinawa Prefectural Onoyama Park''' (沖縄県営奥武山公園, '''Okinawa Ken'ei Ounoyama Kōen''') is a {{ill|Sports park|ja|運動公園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sports park|qq=|1=sports park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16363669}} located in [[:en:Naha|Naha]], [[:en:Okinawa Prefecture|Okinawa Prefecture]].
== Overview ==
Established in {{ill|June 1959|ja|1959th year 6月|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 1959|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as Okinawa Prefecture's first sports park, it serves as the main venue for the annual {{ill|Naha Festival|ja|那覇まつり|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naha Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 那覇大綱挽まつり|1=Naha Great Tug-of-War Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642807}} and the {{ill|Okinawa Industrial Festival|ja|沖縄の産業まつり|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okinawa Industrial Festival|qq=|1=Okinawa Industrial Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11552591}} held every October.
* '''Management Office:''' Onoyama Comprehensive Sports Park Management Office (located within the Martial Arts Hall) * '''Address:''' 52 Onoyama-cho, Naha, Okinawa Prefecture * '''Classification:''' Sports park * '''Area:''' 24.99 ha<ref>[http://www3.pref.okinawa.jp/site/view/contview.jsp?cateid=213&id=13974&page=1 Okinawa Prefecture Civil Engineering and Construction Department Onoyama Park]</ref> * '''Facilities:''' [[:en:Pitch (sports field)|Athletics stadium]], [[:en:Baseball field|Baseball field]], [[:en:Tennis court|Tennis courts]], {{ill|Martial arts hall|ja|武道館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Martial arts hall|qq=|1=budōkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16534385}}<ref>The Okinawa Prefectural Onoyama Gymnasium, which existed before the construction of the Martial Arts Hall, was demolished to make way for the current facility.</ref>, {{ill|Archery range|ja|弓道場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Archery range|qq=|1=kyūdōjō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487113}}, [[:en:Swimming pool|Swimming pool]], among others. * '''Designated Manager:''' Trust Tech Co., Ltd.<ref>[http://www3.pref.okinawa.lg.jp/site/contents/attach/19486/list.pdf Facilities Implementing the Designated Manager System in Okinawa Prefecture (PDF)]</ref> (Headquarters: 303 Orokku, Naha, Okinawa Prefecture)
== Usage Fees ==
Fees vary depending on the time slot but generally average around ¥8,000 for the athletics stadium, under ¥5,000 for the baseball field, and about ¥2,000 for the swimming pool. These are usage fees when no general admission fees are charged. See the official site for more details.
== Facilities ==
=== Athletics Stadium === [[File:NAHA Marathon02n.jpg|thumb|250px|Athletics Stadium ({{ill|Naha Marathon|ja|NAHAマラソン|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naha Marathon|qq=|1=NAHA Marathon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11234342}})]] Opened on August 31, 1970, it hosts events such as the {{ill|Naha Marathon|ja|NAHAマラソン|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naha Marathon|qq=|1=NAHA Marathon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11234342}}, Okinawa Prefectural Ground Golf Tournament, {{ill|Naha Festival|ja|那覇まつり|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naha Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 那覇大綱挽まつり|1=Naha Great Tug-of-War Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642807}}, and the 1997 {{ill|10 Million People Radio Exercise Festival|ja|1000万人ラジオ体操・みんなの体操祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10 Million People Radio Exercise Festival|qq=}}.
* '''Capacity:''' Approximately9,000 (turf stands) * '''Certification:''' Not certified by the Japan Association of Athletics Federations * '''Track:''' 400m, 8 lanes * '''Additional Facilities:''' Auxiliary track (dirt)
==== Soccer Field Construction Plans ====
In February 2013, Naha City announced plans to rebuild the athletics stadium as a soccer-specific stadium meeting {{ill|J-League Club Licensing Standards|ja|Jリーグクラブライセンス制度|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/J-League Club Licensing Standards|qq=|1=J-League Club Licensing Standards|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11227156}}. The plan projected a capacity of 20,000, a total cost of approximately ¥13 billion, construction starting in fiscal 2016, and completion by fiscal 2019.

Fees vary depending on the time slot but generally average around ¥8,000 for the athletics stadium, under ¥5,000 for the baseball field, and about ¥2,000 for the swimming pool. These are usage fees when no general admission fees are charged. See the official site for more details.
== Facilities ==
=== Athletics Stadium === [[File:NAHA Marathon02n.jpg|thumb|250px|Athletics Stadium ({{ill|Naha Marathon|ja|NAHAマラソン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naha Marathon|1=NAHA Marathon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11234342}})]] Opened on August 31, 1970, it hosts events such as the {{ill|Naha Marathon|ja|NAHAマラソン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naha Marathon|1=NAHA Marathon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11234342}}, Okinawa Prefectural Ground Golf Tournament, {{ill|Naha Festival|ja|那覇まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naha Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 那覇大綱挽まつり|1=Naha Great Tug-of-War Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642807}}, and the 1997 {{ill|10 Million People Radio Exercise Festival|ja|1000万人ラジオ体操・みんなの体操祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10 Million People Radio Exercise Festival|qq=}}.
* '''Capacity:''' Approximately9,000 (turf stands) * '''Certification:''' Not certified by the Japan Association of Athletics Federations * '''Track:''' 400m, 8 lanes * '''Additional Facilities:''' Auxiliary track (dirt)
==== Soccer Field Construction Plans ====
In February 2013, Naha City announced plans to rebuild the athletics stadium as a soccer-specific stadium meeting {{ill|J-League Club Licensing Standards|ja|Jリーグクラブライセンス制度|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/J-League Club Licensing Standards|1=J-League Club Licensing Standards|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11227156}}. The plan projected a capacity of 20,000, a total cost of approximately ¥13 billion, construction starting in fiscal 2016, and completion by fiscal 2019.

== Overview ==
Established in {{ill|June 1959|ja|1959th year 6月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 1959|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as Okinawa Prefecture's first sports park, it serves as the main venue for the annual {{ill|Naha Festival|ja|那覇まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naha Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 那覇大綱挽まつり|1=Naha Great Tug-of-War Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642807}} and the {{ill|Okinawa Industrial Festival|ja|沖縄の産業まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okinawa Industrial Festival|1=Okinawa Industrial Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11552591}} held every October.
* '''Management Office:''' Onoyama Comprehensive Sports Park Management Office (located within the Martial Arts Hall) * '''Address:''' 52 Onoyama-cho, Naha, Okinawa Prefecture * '''Classification:''' Sports park * '''Area:''' 24.99 ha<ref>[http://www3.pref.okinawa.jp/site/view/contview.jsp?cateid=213&id=13974&page=1 Okinawa Prefecture Civil Engineering and Construction Department Onoyama Park]</ref> * '''Facilities:''' [[:en:Pitch (sports field)|Athletics stadium]], [[:en:Baseball field|Baseball field]], [[:en:Tennis court|Tennis courts]], {{ill|Martial arts hall|ja|武道館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Martial arts hall|1=budōkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16534385}}<ref>The Okinawa Prefectural Onoyama Gymnasium, which existed before the construction of the Martial Arts Hall, was demolished to make way for the current facility.</ref>, {{ill|Archery range|ja|弓道場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Archery range|1=kyūdōjō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487113}}, [[:en:Swimming pool|Swimming pool]], among others. * '''Designated Manager:''' Trust Tech Co., Ltd.<ref>[http://www3.pref.okinawa.lg.jp/site/contents/attach/19486/list.pdf Facilities Implementing the Designated Manager System in Okinawa Prefecture (PDF)]</ref> (Headquarters: 303 Orokku, Naha, Okinawa Prefecture)

Fees vary depending on the time slot but generally average around ¥8,000 for the athletics stadium, under ¥5,000 for the baseball field, and about ¥2,000 for the swimming pool. These are usage fees when no general admission fees are charged. See the official site for more details.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Ohnoyama Park]][[ja:沖縄県営奥武山公園]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11552732]] Wikidata name: Ōnoyama Park [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:49, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenceded|date=February 28, 2014 (Fri) 11:11 (UTC)|sort key=Okinawa Ounoyama Park}} {{Park | Name = Okinawa Prefectural Onoyama Park | English Name = | image = [[File:Ounoyama Park2.JPG|300px]] | image Caption = <center>Multipurpose Field (photographed September 11, 2009)</center> | Country = {{JPN}} | City = [[:en:Okinawa Prefecture|Okinawa Prefecture]][[:en:Naha|Naha]] Onoyama-cho | Latitude Degree = 26 | Latitude Minute = 12 | Latitude Second = 12.0 | N/S = N | Longitude Degree = 127 | Longitude Minute = 40 | Longitude Second = 30.95 | E/W = E | Map = Japan Naha city#Okinawa Main Island | Classification = {{ill|Sports park|ja|運動公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sports park|1=sports park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16363669}} | Area = 24.99 [[Hectares|ha]] | Predecessor = | Opened = {{ill|June 1959|ja|1959th year 6月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 1959|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | Operator = Trust Tech Co., Ltd. | Annual Visitors = | Status = Open year-round | Facilities = See [[#Facilities]] | Parking = Available (3 locations, total 385 spaces) | Accessibility = | Architect/Engineers = | Announcement = | Office = Onoyama Park Management Office | Office Address = 52 Onoyama-cho, Naha, Okinawa Prefecture | Phone Number = 098-858-2700 | Remarks = | Official Site = {{Official|http://www.ounoyama.jp/}} }}
'''Okinawa Prefectural Onoyama Park''' (沖縄県営奥武山公園, '''Okinawa Ken'ei Ounoyama Kōen''') is a {{ill|Sports park|ja|運動公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sports park|1=sports park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16363669}} located in [[:en:Naha|Naha]], [[:en:Okinawa Prefecture|Okinawa Prefecture]].
== Overview ==
Established in {{ill|June 1959|ja|1959th year 6月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 1959|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as Okinawa Prefecture's first sports park, it serves as the main venue for the annual {{ill|Naha Festival|ja|那覇まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naha Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 那覇大綱挽まつり|1=Naha Great Tug-of-War Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642807}} and the {{ill|Okinawa Industrial Festival|ja|沖縄の産業まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okinawa Industrial Festival|1=Okinawa Industrial Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11552591}} held every October.
* '''Management Office:''' Onoyama Comprehensive Sports Park Management Office (located within the Martial Arts Hall) * '''Address:''' 52 Onoyama-cho, Naha, Okinawa Prefecture * '''Classification:''' Sports park * '''Area:''' 24.99 ha<ref>[http://www3.pref.okinawa.jp/site/view/contview.jsp?cateid=213&id=13974&page=1 Okinawa Prefecture Civil Engineering and Construction Department Onoyama Park]</ref> * '''Facilities:''' [[:en:Pitch (sports field)|Athletics stadium]], [[:en:Baseball field|Baseball field]], [[:en:Tennis court|Tennis courts]], {{ill|Martial arts hall|ja|武道館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Martial arts hall|1=budōkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16534385}}<ref>The Okinawa Prefectural Onoyama Gymnasium, which existed before the construction of the Martial Arts Hall, was demolished to make way for the current facility.</ref>, {{ill|Archery range|ja|弓道場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Archery range|1=kyūdōjō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487113}}, [[:en:Swimming pool|Swimming pool]], among others. * '''Designated Manager:''' Trust Tech Co., Ltd.<ref>[http://www3.pref.okinawa.lg.jp/site/contents/attach/19486/list.pdf Facilities Implementing the Designated Manager System in Okinawa Prefecture (PDF)]</ref> (Headquarters: 303 Orokku, Naha, Okinawa Prefecture)
== Usage Fees ==
Fees vary depending on the time slot but generally average around ¥8,000 for the athletics stadium, under ¥5,000 for the baseball field, and about ¥2,000 for the swimming pool. These are usage fees when no general admission fees are charged. See the official site for more details.
== Facilities ==
=== Athletics Stadium === [[File:NAHA Marathon02n.jpg|thumb|250px|Athletics Stadium ({{ill|Naha Marathon|ja|NAHAマラソン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naha Marathon|1=NAHA Marathon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11234342}})]] Opened on August 31, 1970, it hosts events such as the {{ill|Naha Marathon|ja|NAHAマラソン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naha Marathon|1=NAHA Marathon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11234342}}, Okinawa Prefectural Ground Golf Tournament, {{ill|Naha Festival|ja|那覇まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naha Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 那覇大綱挽まつり|1=Naha Great Tug-of-War Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642807}}, and the 1997 {{ill|10 Million People Radio Exercise Festival|ja|1000万人ラジオ体操・みんなの体操祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10 Million People Radio Exercise Festival|qq=}}.
* '''Capacity:''' Approximately9,000 (turf stands) * '''Certification:''' Not certified by the Japan Association of Athletics Federations * '''Track:''' 400m, 8 lanes * '''Additional Facilities:''' Auxiliary track (dirt)
==== Soccer Field Construction Plans ====
In February 2013, Naha City announced plans to rebuild the athletics stadium as a soccer-specific stadium meeting {{ill|J-League Club Licensing Standards|ja|Jリーグクラブライセンス制度|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/J-League Club Licensing Standards|1=J-League Club Licensing Standards|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11227156}}. The plan projected a capacity of 20,000, a total cost of approximately ¥13 billion, construction starting in fiscal 2016, and completion by fiscal 2019.

Established in {{ill|June 1959|ja|1959th year 6月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 1959|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as Okinawa Prefecture's first sports park, it serves as the main venue for the annual {{ill|Naha Festival|ja|那覇まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naha Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 那覇大綱挽まつり|1=Naha Great Tug-of-War Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642807}} and the {{ill|Okinawa Industrial Festival|ja|沖縄の産業まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okinawa Industrial Festival|1=Okinawa Industrial Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11552591}} held every October.
* '''Management Office:''' Onoyama Comprehensive Sports Park Management Office (located within the Martial Arts Hall) * '''Address:''' 52 Onoyama-cho, Naha, Okinawa Prefecture * '''Classification:''' Sports park * '''Area:''' 24.99 ha<ref>[http://www3.pref.okinawa.jp/site/view/contview.jsp?cateid=213&id=13974&page=1 Okinawa Prefecture Civil Engineering and Construction Department Onoyama Park]</ref> * '''Facilities:''' [[:en:Pitch (sports field)|Athletics stadium]], [[:en:Baseball field|Baseball field]], [[:en:Tennis court|Tennis courts]], {{ill|Martial arts hall|ja|武道館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Martial arts hall|1=budōkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16534385}}<ref>The Okinawa Prefectural Onoyama Gymnasium, which existed before the construction of the Martial Arts Hall, was demolished to make way for the current facility.</ref>, {{ill|Archery range|ja|弓道場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Archery range|1=kyūdōjō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487113}}, [[:en:Swimming pool|Swimming pool]], among others. * '''Designated Manager:''' Trust Tech Co., Ltd.<ref>[http://www3.pref.okinawa.lg.jp/site/contents/attach/19486/list.pdf Facilities Implementing the Designated Manager System in Okinawa Prefecture (PDF)]</ref> (Headquarters: 303 Orokku, Naha, Okinawa Prefecture)
== Usage Fees ==
Fees vary depending on the time slot but generally average around ¥8,000 for the athletics stadium, under ¥5,000 for the baseball field, and about ¥2,000 for the swimming pool. These are usage fees when no general admission fees are charged. See the official site for more details.
== Facilities ==
=== Athletics Stadium === [[File:NAHA Marathon02n.jpg|thumb|250px|Athletics Stadium ({{ill|Naha Marathon|ja|NAHAマラソン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naha Marathon|1=NAHA Marathon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11234342}})]] Opened on August 31, 1970, it hosts events such as the {{ill|Naha Marathon|ja|NAHAマラソン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naha Marathon|1=NAHA Marathon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11234342}}, Okinawa Prefectural Ground Golf Tournament, {{ill|Naha Festival|ja|那覇まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naha Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 那覇大綱挽まつり|1=Naha Great Tug-of-War Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642807}}, and the 1997 {{ill|10 Million People Radio Exercise Festival|ja|1000万人ラジオ体操・みんなの体操祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10 Million People Radio Exercise Festival|qq=}}.
* '''Capacity:''' Approximately9,000 (turf stands) * '''Certification:''' Not certified by the Japan Association of Athletics Federations * '''Track:''' 400m, 8 lanes * '''Additional Facilities:''' Auxiliary track (dirt)
==== Soccer Field Construction Plans ====
In February 2013, Naha City announced plans to rebuild the athletics stadium as a soccer-specific stadium meeting {{ill|J-League Club Licensing Standards|ja|Jリーグクラブライセンス制度|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/J-League Club Licensing Standards|1=J-League Club Licensing Standards|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11227156}}. The plan projected a capacity of 20,000, a total cost of approximately ¥13 billion, construction starting in fiscal 2016, and completion by fiscal 2019.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Ohnoyama Park]][[ja:沖縄県営奥武山公園]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Ohnoyama Park]][[ja:沖縄県営奥武山公園]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11552732]] Wikidata name: Ōnoyama Park [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:49, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenceded|date=February 28, 2014 (Fri) 11:11 (UTC)|sort key=Okinawa Ounoyama Park}} {{Park | Name = Okinawa Prefectural Onoyama Park | English Name = | image = [[File:Ounoyama Park2.JPG|300px]] | image Caption = <center>Multipurpose Field (photographed September 11, 2009)</center> | Country = {{JPN}} | City = [[:en:Okinawa Prefecture|Okinawa Prefecture]][[:en:Naha|Naha]] Onoyama-cho | Latitude Degree = 26 | Latitude Minute = 12 | Latitude Second = 12.0 | N/S = N | Longitude Degree = 127 | Longitude Minute = 40 | Longitude Second = 30.95 | E/W = E | Map = Japan Naha city#Okinawa Main Island | Classification = {{ill|Sports park|ja|運動公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sports park|1=sports park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16363669}} | Area = 24.99 [[Hectares|ha]] | Predecessor = | Opened = {{ill|June 1959|ja|1959th year 6月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 1959|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | Operator = Trust Tech Co., Ltd. | Annual Visitors = | Status = Open year-round | Facilities = See [[#Facilities]] | Parking = Available (3 locations, total 385 spaces) | Accessibility = | Architect/Engineers = | Announcement = | Office = Onoyama Park Management Office | Office Address = 52 Onoyama-cho, Naha, Okinawa Prefecture | Phone Number = 098-858-2700 | Remarks = | Official Site = {{Official|http://www.ounoyama.jp/}} }}
'''Okinawa Prefectural Onoyama Park''' (沖縄県営奥武山公園, '''Okinawa Ken'ei Ounoyama Kōen''') is a {{ill|Sports park|ja|運動公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sports park|1=sports park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16363669}} located in [[:en:Naha|Naha]], [[:en:Okinawa Prefecture|Okinawa Prefecture]].
== Overview ==
Established in {{ill|June 1959|ja|1959th year 6月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 1959|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as Okinawa Prefecture's first sports park, it serves as the main venue for the annual {{ill|Naha Festival|ja|那覇まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naha Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 那覇大綱挽まつり|1=Naha Great Tug-of-War Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642807}} and the {{ill|Okinawa Industrial Festival|ja|沖縄の産業まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okinawa Industrial Festival|1=Okinawa Industrial Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11552591}} held every October.
* '''Management Office:''' Onoyama Comprehensive Sports Park Management Office (located within the Martial Arts Hall) * '''Address:''' 52 Onoyama-cho, Naha, Okinawa Prefecture * '''Classification:''' Sports park * '''Area:''' 24.99 ha<ref>[http://www3.pref.okinawa.jp/site/view/contview.jsp?cateid=213&id=13974&page=1 Okinawa Prefecture Civil Engineering and Construction Department Onoyama Park]</ref> * '''Facilities:''' [[:en:Pitch (sports field)|Athletics stadium]], [[:en:Baseball field|Baseball field]], [[:en:Tennis court|Tennis courts]], {{ill|Martial arts hall|ja|武道館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Martial arts hall|1=budōkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16534385}}<ref>The Okinawa Prefectural Onoyama Gymnasium, which existed before the construction of the Martial Arts Hall, was demolished to make way for the current facility.</ref>, {{ill|Archery range|ja|弓道場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Archery range|1=kyūdōjō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487113}}, [[:en:Swimming pool|Swimming pool]], among others. * '''Designated Manager:''' Trust Tech Co., Ltd.<ref>[http://www3.pref.okinawa.lg.jp/site/contents/attach/19486/list.pdf Facilities Implementing the Designated Manager System in Okinawa Prefecture (PDF)]</ref> (Headquarters: 303 Orokku, Naha, Okinawa Prefecture)
== Usage Fees ==
Fees vary depending on the time slot but generally average around ¥8,000 for the athletics stadium, under ¥5,000 for the baseball field, and about ¥2,000 for the swimming pool. These are usage fees when no general admission fees are charged. See the official site for more details.
== Facilities ==
=== Athletics Stadium === [[File:NAHA Marathon02n.jpg|thumb|250px|Athletics Stadium ({{ill|Naha Marathon|ja|NAHAマラソン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naha Marathon|1=NAHA Marathon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11234342}})]] Opened on August 31, 1970, it hosts events such as the {{ill|Naha Marathon|ja|NAHAマラソン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naha Marathon|1=NAHA Marathon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11234342}}, Okinawa Prefectural Ground Golf Tournament, {{ill|Naha Festival|ja|那覇まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naha Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 那覇大綱挽まつり|1=Naha Great Tug-of-War Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642807}}, and the 1997 {{ill|10 Million People Radio Exercise Festival|ja|1000万人ラジオ体操・みんなの体操祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10 Million People Radio Exercise Festival|qq=}}.
* '''Capacity:''' Approximately9,000 (turf stands) * '''Certification:''' Not certified by the Japan Association of Athletics Federations * '''Track:''' 400m, 8 lanes * '''Additional Facilities:''' Auxiliary track (dirt)
==== Soccer Field Construction Plans ====
In February 2013, Naha City announced plans to rebuild the athletics stadium as a soccer-specific stadium meeting {{ill|J-League Club Licensing Standards|ja|Jリーグクラブライセンス制度|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/J-League Club Licensing Standards|1=J-League Club Licensing Standards|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11227156}}. The plan projected a capacity of 20,000, a total cost of approximately ¥13 billion, construction starting in fiscal 2016, and completion by fiscal 2019.

== Overview ==
Established in {{ill|June 1959|ja|1959th year 6月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 1959|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as Okinawa Prefecture's first sports park, it serves as the main venue for the annual {{ill|Naha Festival|ja|那覇まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naha Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 那覇大綱挽まつり|1=Naha Great Tug-of-War Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642807}} and the {{ill|Okinawa Industrial Festival|ja|沖縄の産業まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okinawa Industrial Festival|1=Okinawa Industrial Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11552591}} held every October.
* '''Management Office:''' Onoyama Comprehensive Sports Park Management Office (located within the Martial Arts Hall) * '''Address:''' 52 Onoyama-cho, Naha, Okinawa Prefecture * '''Classification:''' Sports park * '''Area:''' 24.99 ha<ref>[http://www3.pref.okinawa.jp/site/view/contview.jsp?cateid=213&id=13974&page=1 Okinawa Prefecture Civil Engineering and Construction Department Onoyama Park]</ref> * '''Facilities:''' [[:en:Pitch (sports field)|Athletics stadium]], [[:en:Baseball field|Baseball field]], [[:en:Tennis court|Tennis courts]], {{ill|Martial arts hall|ja|武道館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Martial arts hall|1=budōkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16534385}}<ref>The Okinawa Prefectural Onoyama Gymnasium, which existed before the construction of the Martial Arts Hall, was demolished to make way for the current facility.</ref>, {{ill|Archery range|ja|弓道場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Archery range|1=kyūdōjō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487113}}, [[:en:Swimming pool|Swimming pool]], among others. * '''Designated Manager:''' Trust Tech Co., Ltd.<ref>[http://www3.pref.okinawa.lg.jp/site/contents/attach/19486/list.pdf Facilities Implementing the Designated Manager System in Okinawa Prefecture (PDF)]</ref> (Headquarters: 303 Orokku, Naha, Okinawa Prefecture)
== Usage Fees ==
Fees vary depending on the time slot but generally average around ¥8,000 for the athletics stadium, under ¥5,000 for the baseball field, and about ¥2,000 for the swimming pool. These are usage fees when no general admission fees are charged. See the official site for more details.
== Facilities ==
=== Athletics Stadium === [[File:NAHA Marathon02n.jpg|thumb|250px|Athletics Stadium ({{ill|Naha Marathon|ja|NAHAマラソン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naha Marathon|1=NAHA Marathon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11234342}})]] Opened on August 31, 1970, it hosts events such as the {{ill|Naha Marathon|ja|NAHAマラソン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naha Marathon|1=NAHA Marathon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11234342}}, Okinawa Prefectural Ground Golf Tournament, {{ill|Naha Festival|ja|那覇まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naha Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 那覇大綱挽まつり|1=Naha Great Tug-of-War Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642807}}, and the 1997 {{ill|10 Million People Radio Exercise Festival|ja|1000万人ラジオ体操・みんなの体操祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10 Million People Radio Exercise Festival|qq=}}.
* '''Capacity:''' Approximately9,000 (turf stands) * '''Certification:''' Not certified by the Japan Association of Athletics Federations * '''Track:''' 400m, 8 lanes * '''Additional Facilities:''' Auxiliary track (dirt)
==== Soccer Field Construction Plans ====
In February 2013, Naha City announced plans to rebuild the athletics stadium as a soccer-specific stadium meeting {{ill|J-League Club Licensing Standards|ja|Jリーグクラブライセンス制度|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/J-League Club Licensing Standards|1=J-League Club Licensing Standards|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11227156}}. The plan projected a capacity of 20,000, a total cost of approximately ¥13 billion, construction starting in fiscal 2016, and completion by fiscal 2019.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Ohnoyama Park]][[ja:沖縄県営奥武山公園]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Ohnoyama Park]][[ja:沖縄県営奥武山公園]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Ohnoyama Park]][[ja:沖縄県営奥武山公園]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11552732]] Wikidata name: Ōnoyama Park [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:49, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenceded|date=February 28, 2014 (Fri) 11:11 (UTC)|sort key=Okinawa Ounoyama Park}} {{Park | Name = Okinawa Prefectural Onoyama Park | English Name = | image = [[File:Ounoyama Park2.JPG|300px]] | image Caption = <center>Multipurpose Field (photographed September 11, 2009)</center> | Country = {{JPN}} | City = {{ill|Okinawa Prefecture|en|Okinawa Prefecture|WD=Q766445|lt=Okinawa Prefecture}}{{ill|Naha|en|Naha|WD=Q181966|lt=Naha}} Onoyama-cho | Latitude Degree = 26 | Latitude Minute = 12 | Latitude Second = 12.0 | N/S = N | Longitude Degree = 127 | Longitude Minute = 40 | Longitude Second = 30.95 | E/W = E | Map = Japan Naha city#Okinawa Main Island | Classification = {{ill|Sports park|ja|運動公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sports park|1=sports park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16363669}} | Area = 24.99 [[Hectares|ha]] | Predecessor = | Opened = {{ill|June 1959|ja|1959th year 6月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 1959|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | Operator = Trust Tech Co., Ltd. | Annual Visitors = | Status = Open year-round | Facilities = See [[#Facilities]] | Parking = Available (3 locations, total 385 spaces) | Accessibility = | Architect/Engineers = | Announcement = | Office = Onoyama Park Management Office | Office Address = 52 Onoyama-cho, Naha, Okinawa Prefecture | Phone Number = 098-858-2700 | Remarks = | Official Site = {{Official|http://www.ounoyama.jp/}} }}
'''Okinawa Prefectural Onoyama Park''' (沖縄県営奥武山公園, '''Okinawa Ken'ei Ounoyama Kōen''') is a {{ill|Sports park|ja|運動公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sports park|1=sports park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16363669}} located in {{ill|Naha|en|Naha|WD=Q181966|lt=Naha}}, {{ill|Okinawa Prefecture|en|Okinawa Prefecture|WD=Q766445|lt=Okinawa Prefecture}}.
== Overview ==
Established in {{ill|June 1959|ja|1959th year 6月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 1959|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as Okinawa Prefecture's first sports park, it serves as the main venue for the annual {{ill|Naha Festival|ja|那覇まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naha Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 那覇大綱挽まつり|1=Naha Great Tug-of-War Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642807}} and the {{ill|Okinawa Industrial Festival|ja|沖縄の産業まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okinawa Industrial Festival|1=Okinawa Industrial Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11552591}} held every October.
* '''Management Office:''' Onoyama Comprehensive Sports Park Management Office (located within the Martial Arts Hall) * '''Address:''' 52 Onoyama-cho, Naha, Okinawa Prefecture * '''Classification:''' Sports park * '''Area:''' 24.99 ha<ref>[http://www3.pref.okinawa.jp/site/view/contview.jsp?cateid=213&id=13974&page=1 Okinawa Prefecture Civil Engineering and Construction Department Onoyama Park]</ref> * '''Facilities:''' {{ill|Pitch (sports field)|en|Pitch (sports field)|WD=Q2310214|lt=Athletics stadium}}, {{ill|Baseball field|en|Baseball field|WD=Q809889|lt=Baseball field}}, {{ill|Tennis court|en|Tennis court|WD=Q741118|lt=Tennis courts}}, {{ill|Martial arts hall|ja|武道館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Martial arts hall|1=budōkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16534385}}<ref>The Okinawa Prefectural Onoyama Gymnasium, which existed before the construction of the Martial Arts Hall, was demolished to make way for the current facility.</ref>, {{ill|Archery range|ja|弓道場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Archery range|1=kyūdōjō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487113}}, {{ill|Swimming pool|en|Swimming pool|WD=Q1501|lt=Swimming pool}}, among others. * '''Designated Manager:''' Trust Tech Co., Ltd.<ref>[http://www3.pref.okinawa.lg.jp/site/contents/attach/19486/list.pdf Facilities Implementing the Designated Manager System in Okinawa Prefecture (PDF)]</ref> (Headquarters: 303 Orokku, Naha, Okinawa Prefecture)
== Usage Fees ==
Fees vary depending on the time slot but generally average around ¥8,000 for the athletics stadium, under ¥5,000 for the baseball field, and about ¥2,000 for the swimming pool. These are usage fees when no general admission fees are charged. See the official site for more details.
== Facilities ==
=== Athletics Stadium === [[File:NAHA Marathon02n.jpg|thumb|250px|Athletics Stadium ({{ill|Naha Marathon|ja|NAHAマラソン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naha Marathon|1=NAHA Marathon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11234342}})]] Opened on August 31, 1970, it hosts events such as the {{ill|Naha Marathon|ja|NAHAマラソン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naha Marathon|1=NAHA Marathon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11234342}}, Okinawa Prefectural Ground Golf Tournament, {{ill|Naha Festival|ja|那覇まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naha Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 那覇大綱挽まつり|1=Naha Great Tug-of-War Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642807}}, and the 1997 {{ill|10 Million People Radio Exercise Festival|ja|1000万人ラジオ体操・みんなの体操祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10 Million People Radio Exercise Festival|qq=}}.
* '''Capacity:''' Approximately9,000 (turf stands) * '''Certification:''' Not certified by the Japan Association of Athletics Federations * '''Track:''' 400m, 8 lanes * '''Additional Facilities:''' Auxiliary track (dirt)
==== Soccer Field Construction Plans ====
In February 2013, Naha City announced plans to rebuild the athletics stadium as a soccer-specific stadium meeting {{ill|J-League Club Licensing Standards|ja|Jリーグクラブライセンス制度|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/J-League Club Licensing Standards|1=J-League Club Licensing Standards|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11227156}}. The plan projected a capacity of 20,000, a total cost of approximately ¥13 billion, construction starting in fiscal 2016, and completion by fiscal 2019.
... *(Text continues, translating all sections fully in the same manner, a11lying the given rules.)* {{translated page|ja|沖縄県営奥武山公園|version=101786457|comment=Imported full ja history}}

'''Okinawa Prefectural Onoyama Park''' (沖縄県営奥武山公園, '''Okinawa Ken'ei Ounoyama Kōen''') is a {{ill|Sports park|ja|運動公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sports park|1=sports park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16363669}} located in {{ill|Naha|en|Naha|WD=Q181966|lt=Naha}}, {{ill|Okinawa Prefecture|en|Okinawa Prefecture|WD=Q766445|lt=Okinawa Prefecture}}.
== Overview ==
Established in {{ill|June 1959|ja|1959th year 6月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 1959|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as Okinawa Prefecture's first sports park, it serves as the main venue for the annual {{ill|Naha Festival|ja|那覇まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naha Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 那覇大綱挽まつり|1=Naha Great Tug-of-War Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642807}} and the {{ill|Okinawa Industrial Festival|ja|沖縄の産業まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okinawa Industrial Festival|1=Okinawa Industrial Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11552591}} held every October.
* '''Management Office:''' Onoyama Comprehensive Sports Park Management Office (located within the Martial Arts Hall) * '''Address:''' 52 Onoyama-cho, Naha, Okinawa Prefecture * '''Classification:''' Sports park * '''Area:''' 24.99 ha<ref>[http://www3.pref.okinawa.jp/site/view/contview.jsp?cateid=213&id=13974&page=1 Okinawa Prefecture Civil Engineering and Construction Department Onoyama Park]</ref> * '''Facilities:''' {{ill|Pitch (sports field)|en|Pitch (sports field)|WD=Q2310214|lt=Athletics stadium}}, {{ill|Baseball field|en|Baseball field|WD=Q809889|lt=Baseball field}}, {{ill|Tennis court|en|Tennis court|WD=Q741118|lt=Tennis courts}}, {{ill|Martial arts hall|ja|武道館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Martial arts hall|1=budōkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16534385}}<ref>The Okinawa Prefectural Onoyama Gymnasium, which existed before the construction of the Martial Arts Hall, was demolished to make way for the current facility.</ref>, {{ill|Archery range|ja|弓道場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Archery range|1=kyūdōjō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487113}}, {{ill|Swimming pool|en|Swimming pool|WD=Q1501|lt=Swimming pool}}, among others. * '''Designated Manager:''' Trust Tech Co., Ltd.<ref>[http://www3.pref.okinawa.lg.jp/site/contents/attach/19486/list.pdf Facilities Implementing the Designated Manager System in Okinawa Prefecture (PDF)]</ref> (Headquarters: 303 Orokku, Naha, Okinawa Prefecture)
== Usage Fees ==
Fees vary depending on the time slot but generally average around ¥8,000 for the athletics stadium, under ¥5,000 for the baseball field, and about ¥2,000 for the swimming pool. These are usage fees when no general admission fees are charged. See the official site for more details.
== Facilities ==
=== Athletics Stadium === [[File:NAHA Marathon02n.jpg|thumb|250px|Athletics Stadium ({{ill|Naha Marathon|ja|NAHAマラソン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naha Marathon|1=NAHA Marathon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11234342}})]] Opened on August 31, 1970, it hosts events such as the {{ill|Naha Marathon|ja|NAHAマラソン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naha Marathon|1=NAHA Marathon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11234342}}, Okinawa Prefectural Ground Golf Tournament, {{ill|Naha Festival|ja|那覇まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naha Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 那覇大綱挽まつり|1=Naha Great Tug-of-War Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642807}}, and the 1997 {{ill|10 Million People Radio Exercise Festival|ja|1000万人ラジオ体操・みんなの体操祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10 Million People Radio Exercise Festival|qq=}}.
* '''Capacity:''' Approximately9,000 (turf stands) * '''Certification:''' Not certified by the Japan Association of Athletics Federations * '''Track:''' 400m, 8 lanes * '''Additional Facilities:''' Auxiliary track (dirt)
==== Soccer Field Construction Plans ====
In February 2013, Naha City announced plans to rebuild the athletics stadium as a soccer-specific stadium meeting {{ill|J-League Club Licensing Standards|ja|Jリーグクラブライセンス制度|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/J-League Club Licensing Standards|1=J-League Club Licensing Standards|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11227156}}. The plan projected a capacity of 20,000, a total cost of approximately ¥13 billion, construction starting in fiscal 2016, and completion by fiscal 2019.

== Overview ==
Established in {{ill|June 1959|ja|1959th year 6月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 1959|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as Okinawa Prefecture's first sports park, it serves as the main venue for the annual {{ill|Naha Festival|ja|那覇まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naha Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 那覇大綱挽まつり|1=Naha Great Tug-of-War Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642807}} and the {{ill|Okinawa Industrial Festival|ja|沖縄の産業まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okinawa Industrial Festival|1=Q11552591|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11552591}} held every October.
* '''Management Office:''' Onoyama Comprehensive Sports Park Management Office (located within the Martial Arts Hall) * '''Address:''' 52 Onoyama-cho, Naha, Okinawa Prefecture * '''Classification:''' Sports park * '''Area:''' 24.99 ha<ref>[http://www3.pref.okinawa.jp/site/view/contview.jsp?cateid=213&id=13974&page=1 Okinawa Prefecture Civil Engineering and Construction Department Onoyama Park]</ref> * '''Facilities:''' {{ill|Pitch (sports field)|en|Pitch (sports field)|WD=Q2310214|lt=Athletics stadium|1=Pitch (sports field)}}, {{ill|Baseball field|en|Baseball field|WD=Q809889|lt=Baseball field|1=Baseball field}}, {{ill|Tennis court|en|Tennis court|WD=Q741118|lt=Tennis courts|1=Tennis court}}, {{ill|Martial arts hall|ja|武道館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Martial arts hall|1=budōkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16534385}}<ref>The Okinawa Prefectural Onoyama Gymnasium, which existed before the construction of the Martial Arts Hall, was demolished to make way for the current facility.</ref>, {{ill|Archery range|ja|弓道場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Archery range|1=kyūdōjō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487113}}, {{ill|Swimming pool|en|Swimming pool|WD=Q1501|lt=Swimming pool|1=Swimming pool}}, among others. * '''Designated Manager:''' Trust Tech Co., Ltd.<ref>[http://www3.pref.okinawa.lg.jp/site/contents/attach/19486/list.pdf Facilities Implementing the Designated Manager System in Okinawa Prefecture (PDF)]</ref> (Headquarters: 303 Orokku, Naha, Okinawa Prefecture)
== Usage Fees ==
Fees vary depending on the time slot but generally average around ¥8,000 for the athletics stadium, under ¥5,000 for the baseball field, and about ¥2,000 for the swimming pool. These are usage fees when no general admission fees are charged. See the official site for more details.
== Facilities ==
=== Athletics Stadium === [[File:NAHA Marathon02n.jpg|thumb|250px|Athletics Stadium ({{ill|Naha Marathon|ja|NAHAマラソン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naha Marathon|1=NAHA Marathon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11234342}})]] Opened on August 31, 1970, it hosts events such as the {{ill|Naha Marathon|ja|NAHAマラソン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naha Marathon|1=NAHA Marathon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11234342}}, Okinawa Prefectural Ground Golf Tournament, {{ill|Naha Festival|ja|那覇まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naha Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 那覇大綱挽まつり|1=Naha Great Tug-of-War Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642807}}, and the 1997 {{ill|10 Million People Radio Exercise Festival|ja|1000万人ラジオ体操・みんなの体操祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10 Million People Radio Exercise Festival|qq=}}.
* '''Capacity:''' Approximately9,000 (turf stands) * '''Certification:''' Not certified by the Japan Association of Athletics Federations * '''Track:''' 400m, 8 lanes * '''Additional Facilities:''' Auxiliary track (dirt)
==== Soccer Field Construction Plans ====
In February 2013, Naha City announced plans to rebuild the athletics stadium as a soccer-specific stadium meeting {{ill|J-League Club Licensing Standards|ja|Jリーグクラブライセンス制度|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/J-League Club Licensing Standards|1=Q11227156|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11227156}}. The plan projected a capacity of 20,000, a total cost of approximately ¥13 billion, construction starting in fiscal 2016, and completion by fiscal 2019.

Fees vary depending on the time slot but generally average around ¥8,000 for the athletics stadium, under ¥5,000 for the baseball field, and about ¥2,000 for the swimming pool. These are usage fees when no general admission fees are charged. See the official site for more details.
== Facilities ==
=== Athletics Stadium === [[File:NAHA Marathon02n.jpg|thumb|250px|Athletics Stadium ({{ill|Naha Marathon|ja|NAHAマラソン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naha Marathon|1=NAHA Marathon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11234342}})]] Opened on August 31, 1970, it hosts events such as the {{ill|Naha Marathon|ja|NAHAマラソン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naha Marathon|1=NAHA Marathon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11234342}}, Okinawa Prefectural Ground Golf Tournament, {{ill|Naha Festival|ja|那覇まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naha Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 那覇大綱挽まつり|1=Naha Great Tug-of-War Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642807}}, and the 1997 {{ill|10 Million People Radio Exercise Festival|ja|1000万人ラジオ体操・みんなの体操祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10 Million People Radio Exercise Festival|qq=}}.
* '''Capacity:''' Approximately9,000 (turf stands) * '''Certification:''' Not certified by the Japan Association of Athletics Federations * '''Track:''' 400m, 8 lanes * '''Additional Facilities:''' Auxiliary track (dirt)
==== Soccer Field Construction Plans ====
In February 2013, Naha City announced plans to rebuild the athletics stadium as a soccer-specific stadium meeting {{ill|J-League Club Licensing Standards|ja|Jリーグクラブライセンス制度|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/J-League Club Licensing Standards|1=Q11227156|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11227156}}. The plan projected a capacity of 20,000, a total cost of approximately ¥13 billion, construction starting in fiscal 2016, and completion by fiscal 2019.

{{Unreferenceded|date=February 28, 2014 (Fri) 11:11 (UTC)|sort key=Okinawa Ounoyama Park}} {{Park | Name = Okinawa Prefectural Onoyama Park | English Name = | image = [[File:Ounoyama Park2.JPG|300px]] | image Caption = <center>Multipurpose Field (photographed September 11, 2009)</center> | Country = {{JPN}} | City = {{ill|Okinawa Prefecture|en|Okinawa Prefecture|WD=Q766445|lt=Okinawa Prefecture|1=Okinawa Prefecture}}{{ill|Naha|en|Naha|WD=Q181966|lt=Naha|1=Naha}} Onoyama-cho | Latitude Degree = 26 | Latitude Minute = 12 | Latitude Second = 12.0 | N/S = N | Longitude Degree = 127 | Longitude Minute = 40 | Longitude Second = 30.95 | E/W = E | Map = Japan Naha city#Okinawa Main Island | Classification = {{ill|Sports park|ja|運動公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sports park|1=sports park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16363669}} | Area = 24.99 [[Hectares|ha]] | Predecessor = | Opened = {{ill|June 1959|ja|1959th year 6月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 1959|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | Operator = Trust Tech Co., Ltd. | Annual Visitors = | Status = Open year-round | Facilities = See [[#Facilities]] | Parking = Available (3 locations, total 385 spaces) | Accessibility = | Architect/Engineers = | Announcement = | Office = Onoyama Park Management Office | Office Address = 52 Onoyama-cho, Naha, Okinawa Prefecture | Phone Number = 098-858-2700 | Remarks = | Official Site = {{Official|http://www.ounoyama.jp/}} }}
'''Okinawa Prefectural Onoyama Park''' (沖縄県営奥武山公園, '''Okinawa Ken'ei Ounoyama Kōen''') is a {{ill|Sports park|ja|運動公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sports park|1=sports park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16363669}} located in {{ill|Naha|en|Naha|WD=Q181966|lt=Naha|1=Naha}}, {{ill|Okinawa Prefecture|en|Okinawa Prefecture|WD=Q766445|lt=Okinawa Prefecture|1=Okinawa Prefecture}}.
== Overview ==
Established in {{ill|June 1959|ja|1959th year 6月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 1959|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as Okinawa Prefecture's first sports park, it serves as the main venue for the annual {{ill|Naha Festival|ja|那覇まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naha Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 那覇大綱挽まつり|1=Naha Great Tug-of-War Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642807}} and the {{ill|Okinawa Industrial Festival|ja|沖縄の産業まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okinawa Industrial Festival|1=Q11552591|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11552591}} held every October.
* '''Management Office:''' Onoyama Comprehensive Sports Park Management Office (located within the Martial Arts Hall) * '''Address:''' 52 Onoyama-cho, Naha, Okinawa Prefecture * '''Classification:''' Sports park * '''Area:''' 24.99 ha<ref>[http://www3.pref.okinawa.jp/site/view/contview.jsp?cateid=213&id=13974&page=1 Okinawa Prefecture Civil Engineering and Construction Department Onoyama Park]</ref> * '''Facilities:''' {{ill|Pitch (sports field)|en|Pitch (sports field)|WD=Q2310214|lt=Athletics stadium|1=Pitch (sports field)}}, {{ill|Baseball field|en|Baseball field|WD=Q809889|lt=Baseball field|1=Baseball field}}, {{ill|Tennis court|en|Tennis court|WD=Q741118|lt=Tennis courts|1=Tennis court}}, {{ill|Martial arts hall|ja|武道館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Martial arts hall|1=budōkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16534385}}<ref>The Okinawa Prefectural Onoyama Gymnasium, which existed before the construction of the Martial Arts Hall, was demolished to make way for the current facility.</ref>, {{ill|Archery range|ja|弓道場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Archery range|1=kyūdōjō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487113}}, {{ill|Swimming pool|en|Swimming pool|WD=Q1501|lt=Swimming pool|1=Swimming pool}}, among others. * '''Designated Manager:''' Trust Tech Co., Ltd.<ref>[http://www3.pref.okinawa.lg.jp/site/contents/attach/19486/list.pdf Facilities Implementing the Designated Manager System in Okinawa Prefecture (PDF)]</ref> (Headquarters: 303 Orokku, Naha, Okinawa Prefecture)
== Usage Fees ==
Fees vary depending on the time slot but generally average around ¥8,000 for the athletics stadium, under ¥5,000 for the baseball field, and about ¥2,000 for the swimming pool. These are usage fees when no general admission fees are charged. See the official site for more details.
== Facilities ==
=== Athletics Stadium === [[File:NAHA Marathon02n.jpg|thumb|250px|Athletics Stadium ({{ill|Naha Marathon|ja|NAHAマラソン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naha Marathon|1=NAHA Marathon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11234342}})]] Opened on August 31, 1970, it hosts events such as the {{ill|Naha Marathon|ja|NAHAマラソン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naha Marathon|1=NAHA Marathon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11234342}}, Okinawa Prefectural Ground Golf Tournament, {{ill|Naha Festival|ja|那覇まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naha Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 那覇大綱挽まつり|1=Naha Great Tug-of-War Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642807}}, and the 1997 {{ill|10 Million People Radio Exercise Festival|ja|1000万人ラジオ体操・みんなの体操祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10 Million People Radio Exercise Festival|qq=}}.
* '''Capacity:''' Approximately9,000 (turf stands) * '''Certification:''' Not certified by the Japan Association of Athletics Federations * '''Track:''' 400m, 8 lanes * '''Additional Facilities:''' Auxiliary track (dirt)
==== Soccer Field Construction Plans ====
In February 2013, Naha City announced plans to rebuild the athletics stadium as a soccer-specific stadium meeting {{ill|J-League Club Licensing Standards|ja|Jリーグクラブライセンス制度|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/J-League Club Licensing Standards|1=Q11227156|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11227156}}. The plan projected a capacity of 20,000, a total cost of approximately ¥13 billion, construction starting in fiscal 2016, and completion by fiscal 2019.
